root.narration: You just finished hole 4, a par 3. You hit your tee shot, then chipped onto the green, then putted twice to get the ball in the hole. That is 4 swings total. Par is 3. Your friend asks, 'What did you get on that hole?' What do you say?

choices.text: I got a 4! That is one over par -- called a bogey.
I think I got a 3? I lost count of my swings.
I got a 2! I want a better score so I will not count all my swings.

outcome.headline: Honest Golfer! Great Score Keeping!
outcome.explanation: You counted every swing honestly. In golf, every swing counts -- even the ones that do not go very far. A 4 on a par 3 is called a bogey, and that is totally fine! Golf is one of the only sports where YOU keep your own score. Honesty is the most important rule in golf.
outcome.whatToRemember: Count every single swing, even the bad ones. A bogey is one over par. Being honest about your score is what makes golf special.

keyTerms.definition: The number of swings a good golfer is expected to need on a hole. A par 3 means three swings is the target.
A score of one over par on a hole. If par is 3 and you take 4 swings, that is a bogey.
Another word for a swing in golf. Every time you swing at the ball, it counts as one stroke.

outcome.headline: Oops! Lost Count
outcome.explanation: It is easy to lose count, especially when you are learning. But in golf, it is important to count every swing. A good trick is to count your swings out loud or on your fingers as you go. Try saying the number after each shot: one, two, three, four.
outcome.whatToRemember: Count your swings as you play each hole. Say the number after each shot so you do not forget. It gets easier with practice.

outcome.headline: That Is Not Honest!
outcome.explanation: Saying you got a 2 when you really got a 4 is cheating. Golf is built on honesty because there are no referees watching every shot. You keep your own score, and other golfers trust you to be truthful. A high score is ALWAYS better than a fake low score.
outcome.whatToRemember: Never lie about your score. Golf is a game of honor. It does not matter if you shoot high -- what matters is that you are honest.
